title: Mechanical engineers (professional)
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. (Based on 35 related US occupations.)

Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

tasks.task: Report experimental results by writing papers for scientific journals or by presenting information at scientific conferences. Collaborate with other scientists in the design, development, and testing of experimental, industrial, or medical equipment and procedures. Prepare reports, manuscripts, proposals, and technical manuals for use by other scientists and requestors, such as sponsors and customers. Review scientific proposals and research papers.

Supervise students' research on celestial and astronomical phenomena. Teach physics and astronomy to students. Mentor graduate students and junior colleagues. Serve on professional panels and committees. Conduct question-and-answer presentations on astronomy topics with public audiences. Direct the operations of a planetarium. Teach in colleges and universities. Raise funds for scientific research. Write research proposals to receive funding.

Design computer simulations to model physical data so that it can be better understood. Analyze research data to determine its significance, using computers. Present research findings at scientific conferences and in papers written for scientific journals. Analyze data from research conducted to detect and measure physical phenomena. Perform complex calculations as part of the analysis and evaluation of data, using computers. Develop theories based on personal observations or on observations and theories of other astronomers. Conduct engineering research experiments to improve or modify mining and oil machinery and operations.

Analyze performance of vehicles or components that have been redesigned to increase fuel efficiency, such as camless or dual-clutch engines or alternative types of air-conditioning systems. Test performance of vehicles that use alternative fuels such as alcohol blends, natural gas, liquefied petroleum gas, biodiesel, nano diesel, or alternative power methods such as solar energy or hydrogen fuel cells. Test individual parts and products to ensure that manufacturer and governmental quality and safety standards are met. Calculate required capacities for equipment of proposed systems to obtain specified performance and submit data to engineering personnel for approval. Set up and conduct tests of complete units and components under operational conditions to investigate proposals for improving equipment performance. Adjust, repair, or replace faulty components of test setups and equipment.

Confer with engineers, customers, or others to discuss existing or potential engineering projects or products. Investigate customer or public complaints to determine the nature and extent of problems. Collaborate with other astronomers to carry out research projects. Investigate or test vendors' or competitors' products. Supervise or train project team members, as necessary. Maintain records of drilling and production operations.

Design, implement, maintain, or improve electrical instruments, equipment, facilities, components, products, or systems for commercial, industrial, or domestic purposes. Prepare technical drawings, specifications of electrical systems, or topographical maps to ensure that installation and operations conform to standards and customer requirements. Compile data and write reports regarding existing or potential electrical engineering studies or projects. Prepare specifications, designs, or sketches for machines, components, or systems related to the generation, transmission, or use of mechanical or fluid energy. Develop and modify astronomy-related programs for public presentation.

Develop instrumentation and software for astronomical observation and analysis. Design electrical and mechanical systems for avionic instrumentation applications. Design or modify mining and oil field machinery and tools, applying engineering principles. Prepare layouts of machinery, tools, plants, or equipment. Discuss changes in design, method of manufacture and assembly, or drafting techniques and procedures with staff and coordinate corrections. Prepare parts sketches and write work orders and purchase requests to be furnished by outside contractors.

Conduct field surveys or study maps, graphs, diagrams, or other data to identify and correct power system problems. Inspect completed installations and observe operations to ensure conformance to design and equipment specifications and compliance with operational, safety, or environmental standards. Design, develop, and implement computer applications for use in mining operations such as mine design, modeling, or mapping or for monitoring mine conditions. Investigate or test vendors' or competitors' products and coordinate activities of workers engaged in research, planning, and development.

Conduct statistical studies to analyze or compare production costs for sustainable and nonsustainable designs. Review project instructions and specifications to identify, modify and plan requirements fabrication, assembly and testing. Test machinery and equipment to ensure that it is safe and conforms to performance specifications. Select locations and plan underground or surface mining operations, specifying processes, labor usage, and equipment that will result in safe, economical, and environmentally sound extraction of minerals and ores.

Test metals to determine conformance to specifications of mechanical strength, strength-weight ratio, ductility, magnetic and electrical properties, and resistance to abrasion, corrosion, heat, and cold. Conduct failure analyses, document results, and recommend corrective actions. Design molds, tools, dies, jigs, or fixtures for use in manufacturing processes. Specify and supervise well modification and stimulation programs to maximize oil and gas recovery.

Prepare schedules, reports, and estimates of the costs involved in developing and operating mines. Write technical reports for engineering and management personnel. Supervise, train, and evaluate technicians, technologists, survey personnel, engineers, scientists or other mine personnel. Direct and monitor the completion and evaluation of wells, well testing, or well surveys. Examine maps, deposits, drilling locations, or mines to determine the location, size, accessibility, contents, value, and potential profitability of mineral, oil, and gas deposits.
Test aircraft systems under simulated operational conditions, performing systems readiness tests and pre- and post-operational checkouts to establish design or fabrication parameters.

Fabricate and install parts and systems to be tested in test equipment, using hand tools, power tools, and test instruments. Assemble or disassemble complex mechanical systems. Fabricate new or modify existing prototype components or fixtures. Fabricate or assemble mechanical, electrical, or electronic components or assemblies. Attach wires between controllers. Align, fit, or assemble components or parts, using hand tools, power tools, fixtures, templates, or microscopes.

Analyze energy requirements and distribution systems to maximize the use of intermittent or inflexible renewable energy sources, such as wind or nuclear. Monitor and analyze energy consumption. Verify energy bills and meter readings. Collect data for energy conservation analyses, using jobsite observation, field inspections, or sub-metering. Identify and recommend energy savings strategies to achieve more energy-efficient operation. Investigate industrial accidents, injuries, or occupational diseases to determine causes and preventive measures. Recommend best fuel for specific sites or circumstances.

Inspect or test parts to determine the nature or cause of defects or malfunctions. Document test results, using cameras, spreadsheets, documents, or other tools. Evaluate product designs for safety. Conduct research to evaluate safety levels for products. Prepare and write reports detailing specific fire prevention and protection issues, such as work performed, revised codes or standards, and proposed review schedules.

Analyze test data for automotive systems, subsystems, or component parts. Recommend product or component design improvements based on test data or observations. Perform or execute manual or automated tests of automotive systems or component performance, efficiency, or durability. Conduct or direct testing of air quality, noise, temperature, or radiation levels to verify compliance with health and safety regulations. Review employee safety programs to determine their adequacy.

Train operators, engineers, or other personnel. Conduct training or in-services to educate clinicians and other personnel on proper use of equipment. Train customers or other personnel to install, use, or maintain robots.

Modify, maintain, or repair electrical, electronic, or mechanical components, equipment, or systems to ensure proper functioning. Perform preventive or corrective maintenance on robotic systems or components. Troubleshoot robotic systems and make repairs to robots or peripheral equipment, such as replacing defective circuit boards, sensors, controllers, encoders, or servomotors.

Assist engineers to design, develop, test, or manufacture industrial machinery, consumer products, or other equipment. Assist engineers in the design, configuration, or application of robotic systems. Consult with chemists or biologists to develop or evaluate novel technologies. Confer with medical professionals to assess health risks and to develop ways to manage health issues and concerns.

Develop or implement programs related to the environmental impact of engineering activities. Review architectural, mechanical, or electrical plans or specifications to evaluate energy efficiency. Conduct energy audits to evaluate energy use and to identify conservation and cost reduction measures. Collaborate with manufacturing or quality assurance staff to prepare product specifications, standard operating procedures, user manuals, or qualification and validation reports.

Create mechanical models to simulate mechatronic design concepts. Evaluate the efficiency and reliability of industrial robotic systems, reprogramming or calibrating to achieve maximum quantity and quality. Design or conduct follow-up experimentation, based on generated data, to meet established process objectives.

Prepare written documentation of electromechanical test results. Inspect or monitor energy systems, including heating, ventilating, and air conditioning (HVAC) or daylighting systems to determine energy use or potential energy savings. Maintain service records of robotic equipment or automated production systems.

Develop statistical models or simulations, using statistical or modeling software. Analyze, interpret, or create graphical representations of energy data, using engineering software. Develop training materials related to energy management and environmental considerations.

Inspect installation sites. Check floors of plants to ensure that they are strong enough to support heavy machinery. Design and build safety equipment. Install safety devices on machinery or direct device installation.

Repair, rework, or calibrate hydraulic or pneumatic assemblies or systems to meet operational specifications or tolerances. Prepare energy-related project reports or related documentation. Manage the development, design, or construction of energy conservation projects.
Prepare technical reports of findings or recommendations based on synthesized analyses of test results, and write proposals or articles related to research projects.
Design or develop automated robotic systems to increase production volume, precision, and address environmental applications, such as waste processing or energy generation.

Conduct research, along with other scientists, on the engineering aspects of biological systems, including the design and evaluation of medical equipment or biological processes for commercialization.
Evaluate robotic systems or prototypes, and maintain technical project files while documenting design processes, algorithms, and development changes related to robotics or automation.

Design advanced engineering systems for the automation of industrial tasks, including mechanical systems that integrate robotics with sensors, and maintain knowledge of current regulations and safety standards.

Investigate characteristics such as cost, performance, or process capability of microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) device designs using simulation or modeling software, and propose product designs considering customer requirements.
Supervise technologists, engineers, or technicians, ensuring compliance with specifications and quality standards while conducting tests or inspections to assess equipment and processes
Review and approve designs, calculations, or cost estimates, and document engineering specifics, such as schematics and specifications, ensuring compliance with industry standards.
Participate in preparation of product usage instructions and conduct engineering site audits to collect information for design and safety assessments.
Develop methodologies and standards for testing systems and processes, and conduct analyses to suggest improvements for existing development or manufacturing processes.
Monitor environmental impacts and energy uses in product design and recommend procedures for prevention of hazards in mechanical systems, vehicles, or biomedical applications.
Design and evaluate systems for the control of environmental impacts in engineering applications, and integrate nanotechnology to improve performance or reduce hazards.
Research new materials for innovative engineering applications, particularly in biomedical or energy technologies, and analyze their properties for safety and efficiency.
Document engineering processes and validate designs through prototype testing and quality assurance protocols, while ensuring compliance with relevant regulations and standards.
Create operating instructions for systems in nuclear power contexts, ensuring operations comply with safety standards while maintaining documentation for equipment designs.
Design or oversee construction and operation of mechanical systems or equipment for energy applications such as wind and solar technology, ensuring quality and sustainability standards are met.
Prepare project plans for facility improvements, track inventory, budgets, and oversee compliance with contract obligations.
Implement or test design solutions, and establish procedures or protocols for the integration and troubleshooting of robotic systems and mechanical applications.
Create plans, roadmaps, or layouts for mechanical systems installations or improvements, ensuring adherence to engineering principles and operational standards.
Provide consultation or training on engineering topics, including new technologies or methodologies related to robotics and automated systems.
Provide technical direction or support to installation teams during installation, start-up, testing, system commissioning, or performance monitoring.
Perform computer simulation of solar photovoltaic (PV) generation system performance or energy production to optimize efficiency.
Develop design specifications and functional requirements for solar energy systems or components.
Create plans for solar energy system development, monitoring, and evaluation activities.
Create electrical diagrams and connection diagrams for solar electric systems, using CAD software.
Create checklists for review or inspection of completed solar installation projects.
Design or coordinate design of photovoltaic (PV) or solar thermal systems, including system components, for buildings.
Write reports to document wind farm collector system test results.
Oversee the work of contractors in accordance with project requirements.
Advise on fire prevention equipment and techniques and on fire code interpretation and compliance.
Determine causes of fires and recommend ways to prevent them.
Develop plans for the prevention of damage by fire, wind, and water.
Study the relationships between ignition sources and materials to understand how fires start.
Attend workshops, seminars, or conferences for information regarding fire prevention and protection.

Evaluate fire department performance and relevant laws and regulations concerning fire prevention.

Inspect buildings to determine fire protection system requirements and identify problems.
Design fire detection equipment, alarm systems, and extinguishing devices.
Examine accidents to gather data for preventive design measures.
Monitor nuclear facility operations for compliance with safety regulations.

Plan sequences of calibration tests according to specifications and scientific principles.
